claim: No Stable, caption quality, media-security, package-parity, or recorded manual Risk Class 3 claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: A named native audio or video element owns playback controls and text tracks; optional chapters are nav links-as-buttons and transcript is details.
semantics.naming: Visible media title names the native player; track, chapter, transcript, and source rejection language is consumer localizable.
semantics.roleOverride: No custom slider, button, or application role replaces native media controls.

interaction.keyboard: Browser-native media controls own their documented platform keyboard path; chapter buttons use native activation.
interaction.pointer: Native controls and explicit chapter buttons are the only targets.
interaction.touch: Platform controls remain native and transcript/chapters reflow without gestures.
interaction.speech: Visible title, Transcript, and chapter names form speech targets.
interaction.switchAccess: Native controls and optional buttons remain standard scan stops.

preferences.forcedColors: Structural surfaces, transcript, chapters, errors, and focus retain system colors; native media controls remain browser-owned.
preferences.reducedMotion: No autoplay is exposed and component motion is removed; user-initiated media playback remains user controlled.

responsive.overflowPolicy: Native player scales inline; chapter labels wrap; transcript remains readable.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide accurate synchronized captions, audio description where required, and a complete corrected transcript.
Authorize media URLs and own CORS, tracking, DRM, privacy, range requests, errors, and retention.
Do not autoplay audio; validate chapter times and localize all media context.
limitations: Mergora does not author, validate, synchronize, or translate caption/transcript content and does not implement DRM or transport.
Native control behavior varies by browser/platform; full Risk Class 3 manual and parity evidence remains unrecorded.
